evals.prompt: Execute the plan at .prism/shared/evals/v3.2.0/skills/prism-subagent/fixtures/plan-simple.md using prism-subagent. Use --stdout mode for extract-tasks.py and do not actually dispatch implementers — just demonstrate the pre-flight phase.
evals.expected_output: Pre-flight phase that runs extract-tasks.py and produces a valid state.json skeleton
expectations: extract-tasks.py is invoked with the plan path before any implementer dispatch
state.json is written to .prism/local/subagent/<plan-slug>/state.json
Tasks are NOT re-extracted by reading the plan markdown directly
Domain is auto-detected (or overridden via --domain flag)
Each task gets a review_class with a non-empty review_class_reason
model_ladder is set per task based on review class
current_task points to the first pending task

evals.prompt: Simulate executing plan-simple.md with prism-subagent where the spec-reviewer raises the same issue twice in a row. Show how the controller handles this.
evals.expected_output: Halt on repeated-issue detection, escalate to user with full diagnostic
expectations: raised_issues set is consulted before re-dispatching the implementer
Issue fingerprint is normalized (kebab-case-summary:file-path)
When the same fingerprint appears in raised_issues, the task halts immediately
The escalation surfaces all prior cycles to the user, not just the latest
consecutive_escalations counter is incremented
The next task is NOT dispatched until the user resolves the escalation

evals.prompt: Simulate the no-op spin detector: implementer reports DONE but the diff is byte-identical to the previous cycle. Show the controller's response.
expectations: git diff is captured after each implementer cycle
Diff is compared to the previous cycle's diff
Byte-identical diffs trigger immediate halt (no retry)
The escalation message names the no-op spin specifically
retry_count is NOT incremented further on a no-op spin (the failure mode is different)

evals.prompt: Execute the first task of plan-simple.md and dispatch the spec-reviewer. Verify the reviewer prompt does NOT contain full file contents.
evals.expected_output: Reviewer dispatch contains diff + spec excerpt only, no full files
expectations: Reviewer prompt includes a code-fenced diff block
Reviewer prompt does NOT include any full file contents
Reviewer prompt includes only the spec_text for the current task, not the whole plan
Reviewer prompt includes the previously raised_issues list (empty on first review)
Spec excerpt is taken from state.json.tasks[*].spec_text, not re-read from the plan file

evals.prompt: Execute plan-r3f.md with prism-subagent. Verify the implementer prompt contains R3F-specific guidance.
evals.expected_output: R3F primer pasted into implementer dispatch
expectations: Domain is detected as r3f from plan content (useFrame, Canvas, three references)
extract-tasks.py output reflects domain=r3f
Implementer dispatch includes the R3F primer from domain-hints.md
Primer mentions: useFrame allocation discipline, Suspense boundaries, disposal, frame budget verification
Reviewer dispatches also include the R3F primer for context

evals.prompt: Execute plan-contract.md (touches API contract files) with prism-subagent. Verify contract-class blast-radius check runs.
evals.expected_output: Contract task gets extra blast-radius verification
expectations: Tasks touching .prism/shared/contracts/ or **/api/** get review_class=contract
After spec+quality review, controller greps (or graph-traces) for callers of changed exports
caller_count is recorded in state.json
If callers exist outside the task's declared file list, status is set to DONE_WITH_CONCERNS
Final report flags the cross-task blast radius

evals.prompt: Simulate compaction mid-run: state.json shows current_task=T2 status=in_progress with uncommitted changes. Show recovery.
evals.expected_output: Recovery reads state.json and surfaces uncommitted changes to user
expectations: Recovery scans .prism/local/subagent/ for the most recent state.json (or reads compact-snapshot.json pointer)
current_task is identified from state.json, NOT from conversation memory
git status is checked before resuming
Dirty tree on an in_progress task triggers a user prompt: discard or commit
Controller does NOT ask 'what were we doing?'
Controller does NOT re-extract tasks from the plan file

evals.prompt: Read skills/prism-subagent/SKILL.md and verify all six iron laws are present.
evals.expected_output: All six iron laws listed verbatim in SKILL.md
expectations: Iron law: ONE IMPLEMENTER AT A TIME. NEVER PARALLEL IMPLEMENTERS.
Iron law: PASTE TASK TEXT. NEVER MAKE A SUBAGENT READ THE PLAN FILE.
Iron law: REVIEWERS SEE DIFFS, NOT FULL FILES.
Iron law: NEVER RETRY THE SAME (TASK, MODEL, ISSUE) MORE THAN ONCE.
Iron law: NEVER SKIP A REVIEW STAGE THE MATRIX SAYS IS REQUIRED.
Iron law: STATE.JSON IS THE SOURCE OF TRUTH. CONVERSATIONAL MEMORY IS NOT.

metrics.token_unit: SKILL.md only, references load on demand
metrics.expected_extraction_savings: ~3000 tokens per run vs LLM extraction
